Keep bystanders, children, and pets at least 10 meters away during operation <PIC>. 8. Wear hearing protection — sound levels exceed 85 dBA. 9. Ensure all cables/wires are properly routed before closing enclosures <PIC>. 10. Regularly inspect safety devices, valves, seals, and guards for damage or blockage — replace only with authorized parts <PIC>. 11. Never modify, bypass, or tamper with safety mechanisms — doing so voids warranty and creates serious injury risk. , ["Manual18_3", "Blower_02", "Manual14_0", "Blower_01"]

question: "How do you adjust the intensity settings on an electric toothbrush to suit your oral care needs?"
ret: To adjust the intensity settings on your electric toothbrush to suit your oral care needs: 1. Your toothbrush has 3 manual intensity levels: Low (one light), Medium (two lights), and High (three lights). 2. To change intensity, press the intensity indicator lights on the handle — this cycles through the options before, during, or after brushing. 3. For added gum protection, Adaptive Intensity is pre-activated: if you apply excessive pressure for too long, the toothbrush automatically lowers intensity by one level (with a brief pause and vibration change); it resets to your selected level at the start of each new brushing cycle. 4. You can also customize intensity and other settings via the companion app — though no mode is displayed on the handle, your selection is saved and applied. Note: All intensity adjustments are safe for use with braces, fillings, crowns, and veneers.

question: "What are the steps to activate and deactivate the various features on an electric toothbrush for customized usage?"
ret: To activate or deactivate features on your electric toothbrush for customized usage, follow these steps: # From the handle (manual method): Step 1: Place the handle on the charging stand. Step 2: Press and hold the power button for the required duration (exact timing not specified in reference docs; consult full manual if needed). Note: The Brush head replacement reminder and Pressure Sensor Feedback come activated by default. Adaptive Intensity also ships activated, but is automatically deactivated if Pressure Sensor is turned off. Scrubbing Feedback comes deactivated and must be manually enabled. # From the app (recommended for full customization): 1. Download the official app and ensure Bluetooth is on. 2. Pick up the toothbrush to activate it (lights will illuminate). 3. Open the app and follow guided setup steps to pair and create an account. 4. Complete any firmware update when prompted. 5. Navigate to 'App - Features' to enable or disable: - Adaptive Intensity - Scrubbing Feedback - Intensity Settings (High/Medium/Low — indicated by 3/2/1 lights) - Mode Controls (e.g., all-in-one clean mode with 2-minute BrushPacer) # Additional notes: - To manually adjust intensity during use: press the intensity indicator lights on the handle to cycle through High (3 lights), Medium (2 lights), Low (1 light). - The SenseIQ indicator illuminates when smart features (Adaptive Intensity, Pressure Sensor, Scrubbing Feedback) are active — both during brushing and when enabling/disabling settings. - All personalized settings (mode, intensity, feedback preferences) are stored in the app and synced to the toothbrush upon connection. For full instructions on pairing and syncing, refer to 'App - Getting Started'.

question: "What are the steps to charge an electric toothbrush when it is inside a travel case, and how do you ensure it charges properly?"
ret: To charge your electric toothbrush inside the travel case, follow these steps: 1. Place the toothbrush handle into the travel case. 2. Plug the USB-C cord into the travel case’s USB-C socket and into the USB wall adapter. 3. Plug the wall adapter into a working electrical outlet. 4. Ensure the travel case is placed on its side for stability . 5. Leave the travel case plugged in until fully charged — this may take up to 16 hours . The battery indicator light on the handle will blink while charging and will turn off (stop blinking) when fully charged . To ensure proper charging: make sure the toothbrush handle and brush head are completely dry before placing them in the travel case , and avoid using the toothbrush or charger near water or in wet environments .
